title: 10 best herbal capsules for emphysema
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for emphysema are dietary supplements that contain a combination of herbs and other natural ingredients, designed to help alleviate the symptoms of emphysema, a chronic lung condition. These capsules typically include herbs such as licorice root, lungwort, and lobelia, which are believed to help reduce inflammation, thin mucus, and improve respiratory function. By taking these herbal capsules, individuals with emphysema may experience improved lung capacity, reduced coughing and shortness of breath, and an overall better quality of life. For example, one study found that participants who took a lung-supporting herbal supplement experienced a significant improvement in their lung function and a reduction in symptoms compared to those who did not take the supplement. However, it's important to note that herbal capsules should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in conjunction with a doctor's care.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ma huang capsule helps with emphysema because it contains ephedrine, a naturally occurring alkaloid that functions as a bronchodilator. This means that it can help to relax and widen the airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Emphysema is a chronic lung condition that causes shortness of breath due to damage to the air sacs in the lungs. By improving airflow and reducing inflammation in the respiratory system, ma huang capsules may help to alleviate some of the symptoms associated with emphysema. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
remedy_properties: Bronchodilation: Ma Huang contains ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which help to relax bronchial smooth muscles, widening the airways and improving breathing in emphysema.
Anti-inflammatory: The alkaloids in Ma Huang, such as ephedrine, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of emphysema.
Expectorant: Ma Huang contains vasicine, which helps to thin and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe for emphysema patients.
Vasoconstriction: Ephedrine in Ma Huang causes vasoconstriction, reducing blood flow to the respiratory tract and helping to alleviate symptoms of emphysema.
Immune system support: Ma Huang contains vitamin C and other antioxidants, which support the immune system and help protect the lungs from damage, benefiting emphysema patients.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of the ma huang plant contain ephedrine, a naturally occurring alkaloid that helps to dilate the bronchioles and improve breathing in individuals with emphysema.
Leaves: The leaves of the ma huang plant are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them useful in the treatment of emphysema symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of the ma huang plant contain high levels of phenolic acids and phenylethanoid glycosides, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and improve lung function in individuals with emphysema.

Seeds: The seeds of the ma huang plant are a good source of essential fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects and can help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and improve breathing in individuals with emphysema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy capsule helps with emphysema because it contains compounds that can help relax and expand the airways, making it easier to breathe. It has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can help clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the lungs. Additionally, it has been shown to improve lung function and reduce shortness of breath in people with emphysema. The herb's ability to soothe respiratory symptoms and support overall respiratory health makes it a valuable supplement for those suffering from emphysema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English ivy capsules can reduce inflammation in the airways, helping to alleviate the symptoms of emphysema by reducing swelling and irritation, thanks to the presence of saponins and flavonoids.

Expectorant: English ivy has expectorant properties that can help clear mucus and phlegm from the lungs, providing relief for emphysema patients who experience difficulty breathing due to mucus buildup, attributable to the saponins and essential oils.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of English ivy can help protect the respiratory system from infections, which is crucial for individuals with emphysema as they are more susceptible to respiratory infections, owing to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and essential oils.
Bronchodilator: English ivy can help relax and widen the airways, making it easier for emphysema patients to breathe. This is because of the presence of saponins and terpenes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of English ivy can help protect lung tissue from damage caused by free radicals, which is essential for emphysema patients as the disease causes oxidative stress in the lungs, attributable to the flavonoids and terpenoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of English ivy contain saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of emphysema.

Stems: The stems of English ivy contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs of individuals with emphysema.

Roots: The roots of English ivy contain various saponins and essential oils, which have expectorant and mucolytic properties, making it helpful in clearing mucus from the respiratory tract and improving breathing in individuals with emphysema.

Buds: The buds of English ivy contain high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs of individuals with emphysema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsule helps with emphysema because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme has been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, which may help protect the lungs from further damage. The herb also contains a compound called carvacrol, which has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle, further helping to alleviate symptoms of emphysema. By taking thyme capsules, individuals with emphysema may be able to improve their lung function and reduce the severity of their symptoms.
remedy_properties: Bronchodilator: Thyme capsules can help expand the airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe for individuals with emphysema. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as carvacrol and thymol.
Expectorant: Thyme can help loosen and remove mucus from the lungs, reducing congestion and making breathing easier. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids such as apigenin and luteolin.
Antispasmodic: Thyme has the ability to relax smooth muscle tissue, reducing coughing and spasms in the respiratory tract. This is due to the presence of phenolic compounds such as rosmarinic acid.

Antimicrobial: Thyme has the ability to kill or inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the lungs, reducing the risk of infection. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as thymol and carvacrol.

Antioxidant: Thyme has the ability to neutralize harmful free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs. This is due to the presence of flavonoids such as apigenin and luteolin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used in medicinal capsules for emphysema due to their high concentration of volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used in medicinal capsules for emphysema because they contain flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
Stems: Although less potent than the leaves and flowers, thyme stems are sometimes included in medicinal capsules for emphysema due to their ability to soothe coughs and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cowslip capsule helps with emphysema because it contains anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that may help alleviate symptoms associated with the condition. Antibacterial: Great mullein has the ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria, which can help prevent or treat respiratory infections that emphysema patients are prone to. The rotenone and verbascoside in great mullein exhibit antibacterial properties.

Bronchodilator: Great mullein helps widen the airways in the lungs, improving airflow and making it easier for emphysema patients to breathe. This property is due to the presence of essential oils and saponins.

Seeds: The seeds of great mullein contain mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the respiratory tract, making them useful in medicinal capsules for emphysema.
Buds: Mullein buds are rich in saponins, which have expectorant properties and help to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making them a valuable addition to medicinal capsules for emphysema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with emphysema because it contains substances that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, making it easier to breathe. One of the active compounds in licorice, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. Additionally, licorice may help relax the bronchial smooth muscles, which can help improve overall lung function. However, it's important to note that licorice should only be taken under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can have side effects and interact with certain medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice capsules can reduce inflammation in the airways, which is important for emphysema as it can help alleviate breathlessness and improve lung function. The active constituent, glycyrrhizin, is responsible for this property.

Expectorant: Licorice capsules can help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. This is due to the active constituent, glycyrrhizin, which stimulates the production of mucus and aids in its removal.

Antimicrobial: Licorice capsules have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent and treat respiratory infections, common in emphysema. The active constituent, flavonoids, are responsible for this property.

Antioxidant: Licorice capsules contain antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for emphysema as it can help slow down the progression of the disease. The active constituents, flavonoids and coumarins, are responsible for this property.

Immunomodulatory: Licorice capsules can help regulate the immune system, which can help reduce inflammation and improve lung function in emphysema. The active constituent, glycyrrhizin, is responsible for this property.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce inflammation in the airways of people with emphysema.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory conditions, including emphysema, due to their expectorant and soothing properties.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains flavonoids and coumarins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for emphysema treatment.
Leaves: Licorice leaves are rich in various compounds, including flavonoids, saponins, and volatile oils, which may help reduce inflammation and mucus production in the respiratory system.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsule helps with emphysema because it contains a mucilaginous substance that coats and soothes the mucous membranes of the respiratory tract, providing relief from persistent coughing and difficulty in breathing. The herb also has expectorant properties, which help thin and expel mucus from the lungs, thereby improving lung function. Additionally, it has been suggested that marshmallow may have a protective effect on lung tissue, potentially slowing down the progression of emphysema.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow capsules have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, which can help reduce irritation and inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe for emphysema patients. This property is due to the high mucilage content in marshmallow root.

Expectorant property: Marshmallow capsules can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, which is beneficial for emphysema patients as it can help clear airways and reduce breathlessness. This property is attributed to the flavonoids and polysaccharides present in marshmallow.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of emphysema. The anti-inflammatory effect is mainly due to the triterpene saponins and phenolic acids found in marshmallow.

Antitussive property: Marshmallow capsules can help suppress coughing, providing relief to emphysema patients. This property is due to the high mucilage content, which forms a protective layer over the mucous membranes, reducing the cough reflex.

Antioxidant property: Marshmallow capsules contain antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage, which is beneficial for emphysema patients. The antioxidant effect is attributed to the phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other polyphenols present in marshmallow.

Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain antioxidants and can help protect the lungs from damage, which can be beneficial for those with emphysema.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant contain high levels of mucilage and can help relieve cough and congestion associated with emphysema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry capsule helps with emphysema because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms associated with emphysema. Elderberry is also known to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections that can exacerbate emphysema. Furthermore, elderberry has been shown to have expectorant properties, which can help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, elderberry may help improve lung function by increasing the production of surfactants, which help keep the air sacs in the lungs open and functioning properly.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of elderberries, including anthocyanins and vitamin C, can help protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by emphysema.

Immunomodulatory: Elderberry capsules can help regulate and strengthen the immune system, supporting the body's ability to fight off respiratory infections associated with emphysema, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and vitamins A and C.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat emphysema with herbal capsules is by using a combination of carefully selected herbs that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These herbs can help reduce inflammation in the airways, clear mucus, and improve breathing. Some of the recommended herbs include licorice root, thyme, oregano, and turmeric. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen, especially for those with chronic lung conditions like emphysema. Self-treatment with herbal capsules should never replace prescribed medications or medical treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to emphysema that are treated with herbal capsules include chronic bronchitis and pulmonary fibrosis. Herbal capsules such as those containing butterbur, thyme, and eucalyptus are often used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions. Butterbur has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve breathing, while thyme and eucalyptus have expectorant properties that help clear mucus from the lungs. Additionally, herbal capsules with antioxidants like vitamin C and E can help protect lung tissue from further damage. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
